reasoning: North Korea has not conducted a nuclear test since 2017. While U.S. and South Korean intelligence continually assess that Pyongyang is 'ready to test on short notice' (a status they have maintained since completing tunnel preparations in 2022), there are no immediate signs of an imminent test as of May 1, 2025. Furthermore, the inauguration of Donald Trump in early 2025 shifts the geopolitical landscape; Kim Jong Un may prefer to leverage his existing arsenal for negotiations or diplomatic engagement rather than risk closing those doors with a highly provocative nuclear detonation. Given the extremely short forecasting window of just two months (until July 1, 2025) and the eight-year pause in testing, the base rate for a test occurring in this specific window is very low. With no acute intelligence warnings of an impending test, the probability remains firmly at the lower bound.
